behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at a module boundary (request builder -> API client payload). A pure unit test of string formatting can miss dropped labels during wiring/serialization. No real OS/network behavior is required, and correctness does not depend on model output. Rejected higher levels: no behavior text requires real HTTP effects or LLM judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the classifier module to a fake in-process API client that captures outbound payloads; assert every call includes the full configured taxonomy list exactly once before send.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is response handoff + normalization at module boundary (API adapter -> classifier output). A single-function unit test could miss forwarding bugs in orchestration. No real infrastructure required; stubbed response reproduces failure. Rejected Agentic/System: behavior is deterministic mapping of an invalid label, not model-quality dependent.
behaviors.test_description: Use an in-process fake API returning an out-of-taxonomy label; call public classify() and assert returned value is "unknown" and never the raw unexpected label.

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: Behavior targets deterministic normalization logic for identical input, with no required I/O or cross-module invariant. MOCK-MISS does not apply because failure is local algorithmic determinism. Rejected Integration+: behavior text explicitly scopes to normalization step on identical raw responses.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization function/object repeatedly with the same raw response fixture and taxonomy; assert stable identical final label across runs.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on live AI API outcomes across repeated calls; nondeterministic model behavior is part of the failure surface. Mocked/stubbed responses would hide drift/instability that only appears with real model calls. Rejected Workflow: only repeated single-step classification calls, not a multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run repeated real classification calls for fixed document+taxonomy, record provider raw responses and final labels; assert label changes only when provider response content changes.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Failure occurs at handoff between budget enforcement and dispatch (state/payload must be transformed or blocked before send). A local fake client can catch accidental dispatch-over-budget. No real OS/network is required; no dependence on model output quality. Rejected System/Agentic due lack of real-infra or model-decision requirement in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Configure a small token budget with over-budget input, run classifier with fake API client, and assert policy outcome (truncate or reject) is applied before any outbound send.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: This requires a real provider call and real provider token accounting metadata; mocks can trivially satisfy budget and miss true provider-side counting behavior. Execution path includes real AI API behavior, so deterministic local fakes are insufficient for real-failure detection. Rejected Workflow: single classification call, no multi-step agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real API classification for representative documents, capture provider-reported token usage fields, and assert each call is <= configured budget.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Failure is retry orchestration across module boundaries (error handling, backoff, retry, final return). In-process fake API can reproduce 429/RateLimit responses and verify retry behavior. Real network is not strictly required for this behavior, and correctness does not depend on model output semantics. Rejected System/Agentic accordingly.
behaviors.test_description: Use fake API: first call returns rate-limit signal, second call returns success; assert classifier waits/backs off, retries, and returns successful label instead of bubbling error.
